title: Engineer Hawkes-style pit-contagion and inhibition features
summary: Pit decisions are not independent: rival stops create undercut pressure, teammate stops create stack constraints, and race-wide events create short-lived bursts of stop activity.
rationale: Pit decisions are not independent: rival stops create undercut pressure, teammate stops create stack constraints, and race-wide events create short-lived bursts of stop activity. Temporal point-process literature is built for exactly this self-excitation/inhibition pattern, and your current pressure features are close in spirit but not explicitly event-intensity based.
implementation_hint: Create decayed counts and intensities of prior pit events over multiple horizons for race-wide, team, compound, and position-band cohorts. Add both excitation terms (recent rival/team stops raise hazard) and inhibition terms (recent own-team stop may suppress immediate second stop). Test exponential kernels with short half-lives like 1-3 laps and estimate them only from past laps within fold.
expected_effect: Should improve race-lap coordination signal beyond simple same-lap aggregates, especially around clustered stop windows and reaction stops.
risk: If features accidentally use same-lap or future pit counts they will leak badly; all intensities must be shifted. Gains may be modest if the dataset already encodes most bursts through lap-time deltas.
